calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order To Show Cause Why Defendant Should Not Be Held In Contempt
Off Calendar Aug-22-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: Plaintiff Creditors Trade Association's Motion For Order To Show Cause Why Defendant Should Not Be Held In Contempt is ordered off calendar. The filing was insufficient per C.C.P. Sections 1005 and 1013 which require that motions be filed as well as served 16 court days prior to the hearing. This motion was filed on August 6, 2008. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED (EAH/612)

Notice Of Motion To Compel Answers To Post-Judgment Discovery; And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions;
Off Calendar Oct-03-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Plaintiff Creditors Trade's Motion To Compel Answers To Post-Judgment Discovery is granted. No opposition has been filed. Defendant is ordered to respond within 15 days of notice of this order. Defendant's failure to respond was a misuse of the discovery process. Sanctions are awarded against Defendant in the amount of $340, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-24-2007 Off Calendar
